demo.description: The starting point for your next project is based on MUI. Easy customization helps you build apps faster and better.

messages.codeSent: Code sent. Please check your inbox.

hero.description: Unify Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and CLI tool traffic behind one entry point, with user tokens, model routing, cost quotas, and health monitoring in one control plane.

overview.caption: Gateway Control Plane
overview.title: Bring scattered AI calls
overview.description: Hook starts from a simple principle: unify the entry point, centralize governance, and keep provider differences inside the gateway.

unifiedEntry.title: One entry for multiple AI providers
unifiedEntry.description: Applications keep one API base URL while Hook maps Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, and different request formats at the gateway layer.

accessControl.title: Users, groups, and tokens in layers
accessControl.description: Users, groups, API tokens, and permissions converge in one console so credentials do not scatter across scripts and environments.

quotaControl.title: Cost quotas near real traffic
quotaControl.description: Track tokens, cost, and balance by model, group, and user where requests actually pass through.

providers.caption: Provider Mesh
providers.title: Keep provider differences
providers.description: A unified entry point is more than a proxy. Hook understands endpoints, models, formats, and health before sending traffic to the right provider.

signals.modelMapping: Map public models to provider models
signals.formatRewrite: Rewrite requests and responses by API format

custom.name: Custom
custom.note: Custom endpoints, headers, and body rules

operations.caption: Operations
operations.title: Governance close to
operations.description: An AI gateway should do more than forward traffic. Teams need quotas, audit trails, health, and provider strategy on the same request path.

loadBalancing.title: Smart load balancing
loadBalancing.description: Put multiple provider endpoints into one policy and route by priority, weight, and health status.

quota.title: Cost and quota control
quota.description: Aggregate usage from API tokens to users, groups, and models, and explicitly block real requests when budgets run out.

monitoring.title: Health monitoring and tracing
monitoring.description: Record request success rate, latency, errors, and provider responses so debugging starts from the real chain.

adminConfig.title: Admin-controlled configuration
adminConfig.description: Manage models, providers, prices, menus, and permissions in the dashboard instead of changing code for every strategy update.

useCases.caption: Use Cases
useCases.title: From personal scripts to team platforms

individual.title: Individual developers
individual.description: Manage all AI service credentials behind one local or private endpoint so CLI tools, scripts, and projects do not keep switching provider config.

team.title: Engineering teams
team.description: Share one model catalog and quota policy across projects, then add providers at the gateway without interrupting application code.

admin.title: Platform admins
admin.description: Review request records, provider health, user balances, and model prices in one operational dashboard.

sdk.question: Does Hook require a new SDK in business systems?
sdk.answer: The goal is to keep provider differences away from application code. Existing OpenAI-compatible calls, scripts, and CLI tools can point to the gateway, while provider routing and model mapping stay in Hook.

selfHosted.question: What does self-hosted mean here?
selfHosted.answer: You deploy and manage the gateway, users, tokens, prices, records, and policies. The actual model capabilities still come from Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, or custom provider endpoints you configure.

quota.question: How do cost quotas work?
quota.answer: Hook records tokens, model prices, and user balances on the request path, then aggregates by user, group, or token. When a quota is exhausted, the request is explicitly rejected instead of silently degraded.

monitoring.question: What does health monitoring help solve?
monitoring.answer: It centralizes provider latency, errors, success rate, and request records so admins can identify upstream outages, model mapping mistakes, token issues, or malformed business requests.

cta.title: Unify your AI traffic with Hook
cta.description: Start with the first API token and bring providers, models, quotas, and monitoring into a visible, controllable, auditable gateway.
